headline: Cybercrime Losses Exceed RM2.8 Billion With Scams Dominant
context: PDRM Commercial Crime recorded RM2.8 billion cybercrime losses in 2025 with online scams at 65% of total.

cards.big: Cybercrime cost Malaysians RM2.8 billion in 2025 with scams at 65%
cards.sub: Average victim lost RM48,000 with recovery rate below 12%

cards.big: Macau and love scam syndicates operated 85 call centres targeting Malaysians
cards.sub: Most centres in Cambodia Myanmar and Philippines beyond Malaysian jurisdiction

cards.big: Bank Negara kill switch blocked RM520 million in fraudulent transactions
cards.sub: System requires banks to freeze suspected accounts within 24 hours

cards.big: Only 1,200 of 58,000 cybercrime reports resulted in arrests
cards.sub: Cross-border nature makes prosecution extremely difficult

cards.big: Cybercrime scale tests whether enforcement can match the sophistication of criminal networks
RM2.8 billion in cybercrime losses. Digital enforcement requires cross-border cooperation, not just domestic laws.
